s.m. Meditation, contemplation (especially profound and abstract religious contemplation); divine intuition or discernment; consideration, imagination (esp. that profound and abstract consideration which brings objects fully and undisturbedly before the mind); mental representation of the personal attributes of the divinity to whom worship may happen to be addressed; attention, regard, advertency

On Him fixed be your thoughts, as the acrobat's on bamboo high, Six pitchers on her head, drums play, one object hath her eye. 2. Application ( bichār , 3). Ujjal baran, ādhīntā, ek charan, do dhiyān, Ham jāne tum bhagat ho, nire kapaṭ kī khān! Dohā 3. Respect ( khayāl , 5). dhiyān baṭānā , v. a. To distract attention ( chit baṭānā ). dhiyān pe chaṛhnā , v. n. To take one's fancy. dhiyān denā, yā lagānā , v. n. See chit lānā . 1. To give ear to ( kān lagānā ). 2. To take notice; observe; contemplate; meditate; regard; reflect. dhiyān se , adv. Attentively. dhiyān se dekhnā , v. n. To observe attentively. dhiyān lagnā , v. n. def: part. Seen, observed, perceived, felt, experienced; having seen, &c. (used in comp., e.g. jahān-dīda, jang-dīda, &c.)
more: p.p. ( In comp .) Seen. dīdah , Wom. n. m. 1. The eye (آنکهـ 1.). 1. Hāth kashīdah, chanchal dīdah . Prov. Her work held in her hand, her eyes all wanton rove! 2. Charbānkiyāṅ dekho koi launḍi kī hamārī! Maṭkāti hai kyā kyā, muī dede yeh maṭr se ! Nāznīn. Just only see my slave girl how she wantons with her eyes! See how the wretch just rolls and rolls about those pea-like eyes! 3. Tere dīdoṅ meṅ khāk! Wom. May dust go into your eyes! (May you be blind!). 4. Apne dīdoṅ kī sūṅ! Wom. I swear it by these eyes. 2. Courage ( chhātī , 3.). Huī is bāt meṅ mushhūr Yūsuf sā javāṅ tākā , Nāznīn. Buā, ham aurtoṅ meṅ thā ba ā dīdah Zalīkhā kā. dīde phā nā. yā phāṛ-kar dekhnā , v. n. To look steadfastly; to stare; gaze. dīdah-phaṭī , adj. Having large staring eyes. dīdah-dalel , adj. Wanton; bold; immodest. dīdah-dalelī n. f. Wantonness; boldness. dīdah-dhoī, dīdah-dalel, shokh-dīdah, charbāṅk-dīdah …

1. part. (in compos.) Seen, having seen, or observed or experienced. 2. s. m. The eye. 3. A wanton or impudent eye. 4. more: adj. Good-looking; handsome; conspicuous. Baṛā dīdārū javān hai. dīd na shunīd , adj. lit . nor seen, nor heard. Strange; extraordinary; wonderful.

def: (for ديده بان dīda-bān, which has the same meaning) s. m. f. A watch, sentinel, scout.

subs: (-par), To fix eager or covetous eyes (on), look covetously (at), to covet, to be greedy, to be insatiable.

def: n. f. Greediness; covetousness. Jau sasur kittoī khāë, pai ḍiḍyā lagāë rahatū hai . Rus. This child (called father-in-law by way of abuse) may eat ever so much, he is never satisfied.
s. (dakh.) Door (small), wicket (Harris). ¶ duḍḍī, s. (dakh.) Hutch ( Harris ).

def: adv. corr. of dvi-dhā, q.v.
1˚ (old H.) = diyā, perf. part. of denā; — 2˚ (dialec.) = dvidhā, q.v.
s.f. Fixed state of mind or being, firmness, fixedness, stability.
s.m. The second husband of a woman twice married.
s.f. A virgin widow remarried; an elder sister unmarried having a younger married sister; the childless widow of a brother (whom, under the old law, a surviving brother was required to marry).

n. m. The husband of a widow. didhishū , n. f. 1. A child-widow remarried. 2. An elder sister unmarried whose junior is a bride. 3. The childess widow of a brother whom, under the old law, a surviving brother was required to marry.
s.f. = didishu, q.v.
s.f. Light, brightness, splendour; a ray of light, sun-beam, moon-beam.

n. f. S. तग to go. A pace; step ( paiṅḍ ). ḍig bhar'nā, yā dharnā , v. a. To step out. ḍigār'nā , v. a. To drive away ( tāṛnā , 3.). lambī ḍigeṅ dhar'nā , v. a. To take long steps.
s. m. 1. Quarter, region, tract, side, way, -wards (as, اتردگ uttar-dig, Northwards). The Hindūs reckon ten digs (which are mentioned under دکپال dik-pāl ). 2. Point (of the compass).

adj. & s.m. 'Sky-clothed'; having only the sky or atmosphere for raiment, clad by the regions of space, unclad, stark naked; — a naked person; a naked Hindū ascetic; an order of ascetics of the Jain sect who go naked (if clad in coloured cloth, they are called pītāmbar; if in white, svet-āmbar); a member of that order, a Jain mendicant; an epithet of Śiva (from his being naked), and of certain of his devotees
s.m. The end of space, the horizon, remote distance
s.m. Another region, a distant quarter; aërial space, the atmosphere
s.m. The functions of a dig-pāl or guardian deity of any region

dig-pati, s.m. The regent or guardian of one of the ten regions or points of the compass, or of a quarter of the world (of these guardian deities there are two divisions; 1˚ Astronomical, viz. the Sun, of the east; Saturn, of the west; Mercury, of the North; Mars, of the south; Venus, of the south-east; Rahū, of the south-west; the Moon, of the north-west; and Jupiter, of the north-east; — 2˚ Mythological, viz. Brahma, for upwards; Ananta, for downwards; Indra, of the east; Varuṇa, of the west; Kuvera, of the north; Yama, of the south; Agni, of the south-east; Nirriti, of the south-west; Marut or Vāyu, of the north-west; and Iʼśāna or Śiva, of the north-east)

dig-śūl, s.m. Any inauspicious planetary conjunction (as for the Sun and Venns to be in the west, &c.); see diśā-śūl, s.v. diśā; — dig-gaj, s.m. 'Elephant of the quarter'; a mythical elephant of one of the eight quarters or points (which, with others at different quarters, is supposed to support the world); (met.) great, large, huge
s.m. Point or quarter of the compass, point, quarter, direction
s.m. Subjugation of various countries in all directions; subjugation of extensive tracts of country (whether by arms or controversy), universal conquest
s.m. Invasion of various countries, going forth to conquer the whole world
s.m. Conqueror of many realms, or of the world

more: n. m. S. दिक space, अम्बर covering. One whose only garment is the atmosphere. 1. An epithet of Shiv, or Durga. Jahāṅ na jāko gun lahe, tāhāṅ na tāko ṭhāoṅ, Dhobī bas-kar kā kare digambar ke gāoṅ? Prov. The place where you have naught to gain is not the place for you, What custom has the washerman where folks all naked go? 2. A sect of mendicant Jains who go about naked, as distinguished from Pitambars who wear colored clothes, and the Svetambars who wear white clothes only.
1. adj. Naked. 2. s. m. An order of Hindū asceticks, who go naked: they are worshippers of Siva. 3. A mendicant of the sect of Buddha, going naked or wearing coloured clothes.

v.t. To cause to shake or move, to cause to slip, or stumble, or fall, or swerve (from), or relapse, &c.; to shake (as faith, loyalty, &c.), to loosen, to render unsteady or unstable; to displace, dislocate.
more: (caus. of ڐگنا) v. a. To cause to shake or move.

def: adj. & adv. Other, another, next, following ( = dūsrā); — over again, again; moreover, besides, further.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دیگر (dīgar).

def: adj. Another, other (pl. digarān); — adv. Once more, again
more: adj. Other; others; another. digar-gūṅ , Poet. adj. Of another complextion, or color; altered; changed. Inkī hālat digar-gūṅ hai . He is dying. igar digar , Illit. n. f. Quarrel; strife ( jhagṛā ).

n. m. S. ढौक् to go, H. ragaṛ rub, pag , foot ḍig pace. 1. A way; path; track. 2. A wide track between fields for the passage of cattle. Ḍagar mhārī chhāṅḍo, lalā, bindh jāoge nainan māṅ! Ṭhumrī. O leave my road, dear Sir! or in my eyes you'll threaded be! ḍagar batānā , v. n. To guids; direct one on his way.
adj. See ديگر dīgar, Another, other. دگرگون digar-gūn, adj. Of another complexion or colour; altered, changed.
subs: adj. Of another colour or complexion; altered, changed.

def: adj. Changed in form or figure.
v. n. 1. To go along a road; travel. 2. To go away; to be off. ḍagar ḍagar k. , v. n. To tremble from weakness. aur ḍagar chalnā , v. a. 1. To take another road or way. 2. To cheat; trick; deceive.
more: E. n. m. A pole on which a load is suspended.

def: v.t. To cause to go away, to send or drive away.
v.n. To chatter (as teeth).

n. m. 1. The elephant that supports one of the four quarters of the earth. 2. ( Met .) Great; large. Jānahīṅ dig-gaj ur kaṭhināī; Jab jab bhireūṅ jāë bari āī; Jinke dashan karālan phūṭe: Ur lāgat mūlak iv ṭūṭe . Rāmāyan. As hard as earth-supporting elephant his breast. Each time with mighty strength he fiercely 'gainst him pressed, His dreadful tusks like radish snapped against his chest.
more: (lit. Elephant that supports one of the four quarters of the earth; met. ) Great.

def: s.f. An oblong tank or pond.
more: s. f. A large tank, or reservoir, in form of an oblong square.

def: s.f. An oblong tank or pond.

ety: ḍig˚ = ḍag˚ = S. त्रङ्ग(ति), rt. त्रङ्ग, or त्वङ्ग(ति), rt. त्वङ्ग
def: v.n. To shake, tremble, vibrate, quaver; to move, shift, become displaced; to stumble, slip, fall; to swerve (from), deviate, be shaken or moved (as from one's faith, &c.); to shrink, flinch
more: v. n. 1. To shrink. 2. To shake, to vibrate, to tremble, to quaver, to move.

def: s. m. See دکپال dik-pāl.
s.f. A degree; — decree
subs: To issue a decree, to enforce a decree, to take out execution
s.m. Decree-holder, one who takes out a decree, the person in whose favour judgment has been given
ḍigrī karnā, To grant a decree, to decree, adjudge, award.

def: n. f. Cor. of decree (حکم 6.). ḍigrī ijmālī . A joint decree. ḍigrī akhīr yā qataī , Final decree. ḍigrī iqbālī , n. A decree passed on confession of judgment; a judgment by cognovit. ḍigrī pānā, yā hāsil k. , v. a. To obtain a decree. ḍigrī jārī k . To enforce a decree; to take out execution. ḍigrī jārī karānā , v. a. To sue out execution. ḍigrī jārī karne-vālā , n. The party praying for execution of decree; the party who enforces a decree. ḍigrī-dār , n. Decree-holder; judgment creditor; the person in whose favour judgment has been given. ḍigrī sarsarī , n. f. A summary decree. ḍigrī se bachne kā upāë k . To elude or avoid judgment. ḍigrī shufٴa, yā haq-i-shufa . A decree. ḍigrī gair-vasūlī . An unsatisfied decree. ḍigrī-i-farebī , A fraudulent decree. ḍigrī k., yā denā , v. a. To adjudge; decree; award. ḍigrī ke ijrā meṅ , In the execution of decree. ḍigrī kī illat meṅ , On account of execution of decree. ḍigrī nāfiz k . To enforce a decree. ḍigrī yak-tarfah par muqadmah chhoṛnā , To suffer a default; let judgment go by default.
s.f. pl. (of ḍigrī), Degrees; — decrees.
s. m. The quarter towards which it is deemed unlucky to travel on particular days.

def: s.f. (old H.) = deh, q.v. s.f. (but, about Benares, m., as in Prk. & S.), The body, the person
subs: (˚ha+āt˚), s.m. One who asserts that the body and soul are one, a materialist
(˚ha+an˚), s.m. The end of the body, i.e. death
(˚ha+an˚), s.m. Another body, another embodied state (in the doctrine of metempsychosis)
s.m. 'Resigning or relinquishing the body,' dying; death, voluntary death
part. adj. Separated or detached from the body (as excrement, or the spirit)
(-kī, or -apnī), To cover the person, conceal (one's) wickedness
adj. Occupying or assuming a body, embodied, incarnate
(apnī), To keep up the spirits; to be firm, to steady oneself, recover oneself
s.m. Decay or wasting of the body, sickness, disease
deh-vat, deh-vant, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Furnished with a body, embodied, corporeal; of or belonging to the body; — an embodied creature, a living being, a man ( = dehī)
s.f. 'Departure of the body,' dying, death.

def: (from ده dih ) s. m. A haunt, a place, a dwelling, village.

ety: Pārsī dahi; Pehl. dahyū; Zend daṅhu = S. दस्यु
def: s.m. A village
more: 1. s. m. A village. 2. (in compos. from دادن) part. act. Giving, giver. 3. ¶ dah, adj. Ten. ده چند dah-chand, adj. Tenfold, ten times. ده چندگي dah-chanda-gī, s. f. Tenfold (a word with which they return a salutation). ده در دنيا ستردرآخرت dah dar dunyā sattar dar āḵẖirat, A benediction of faḳīrs, meaning, charity will be returned sevenfold in the next world. (It is probably a corrupt. of Pers. phrase, Dah dar dunyā ṣad dar āḵẖirat, Ten in this world, a hundred in the next). ده دله dah-dil-a, adj. 1. Faithless, inconstant. 2. Brave, bold. ده روزه dah-roz-a, A ten days, a decade. ده مني dah-mann-ī, adj. Holding ten mann. ده يک dah-yak or ده يکي dah-yakī, Tenth part.
subs: s.f. A detailed statement of villages and their assessment
s.f. The revenue assessment of a village.

def: s.m. (but fem. in S.), Point of the compass, quarter, direction, side, way; space, region, tract; (in comp.) -wards (e.g. uttar-dig, 'northwards')
more: n. m. P. musht . A blow with the fist. Aisā ḍuk dūṅgā kī dāṅt nikas paṛeṅge. ḍukkam ḍukkā, ḍukā ḍuk , n. f. Boxing; pugilism. Phr. A stand-up fight; a hand-to-hand fight. ḍukyā'nā , v. a. To give a blow.
subs: adj. & s.m. 'Sky-clothed'; having only the sky or atmosphere for raiment, clad by the regions of space, unclad, stark naked; — a naked person; a naked Hindū ascetic; an order of ascetics of the Jain sect who go naked (if clad in coloured cloth, they are called pītāmbar; if in white, svet-āmbar); a member of that order, a Jain mendicant; an epithet of Śiva (from his being naked), and of certain of his devotees
s.m. The end of space, the horizon, remote distance
s.m. Another region, a distant quarter; aërial space, the atmosphere
s.m. The functions of a dig-pāl or guardian deity of any region

dig-pati, s.m. The regent or guardian of one of the ten regions or points of the compass, or of a quarter of the world (of these guardian deities there are two divisions; 1˚ Astronomical, viz. the Sun, of the east; Saturn, of the west; Mercury, of the North; Mars, of the south; Venus, of the south-east; Rahū, of the south-west; the Moon, of the north-west; and Jupiter, of the north-east; — 2˚ Mythological, viz. Brahma, for upwards; Ananta, for downwards; Indra, of the east; Varuṇa, of the west; Kuvera, of the north; Yama, of the south; Agni, of the south-east; Nirriti, of the south-west; Marut or Vāyu, of the north-west; and Iʼśāna or Śiva, of the north-east)

dig-śūl, s.m. Any inauspicious planetary conjunction (as for the Sun and Venns to be in the west, &c.); see diśā-śūl, s.v. diśā; — dig-gaj, s.m. 'Elephant of the quarter'; a mythical elephant of one of the eight quarters or points (which, with others at different quarters, is supposed to support the world); (met.) great, large, huge
s.m. Point or quarter of the compass, point, quarter, direction
s.m. Subjugation of various countries in all directions; subjugation of extensive tracts of country (whether by arms or controversy), universal conquest
s.m. Invasion of various countries, going forth to conquer the whole world
s.m. Conqueror of many realms, or of the world

Dil meṅ āī ko roke so bhaṛvā . Prov. Who speaks not his mind, a pandar you 'll find. 3. Ek tābūt aur kitne murde, kaṭe kaṭāë kyā dil-gurde! Tāl meṅ pīveṅ kālā pānī, rahe, Zafar, nit unkī nishānī . adj. Interesting; entertaining; pleasing. dil-chalā, dil-chalī , adj. See jī-chalā. dil-chalī , Illit. n. f. Courage; generosity, etc. dil-chor , adj. 1. Inattentive; heedless; negligent. 2. Ravishing; captivating ( chit-chor ). dil hāzīr h. , v. n. To retain one's presence of mind. dil khush k. , v. a. To divert, amuse, cheer, delight or gladden the heart. dil daryā . Bountiful as the sea; munificent. Luṭāyā bigānā māl, bandī kā dil daryāo . Prov. Another's property I've given up to plunder free, Your humble servant's heart is boundless as the sea. dil-dihī , n. f. 1. Encouragement. 2. Consolation. dil dukhā'nā , v. a. To hurt one's feelings. dil dekhnā , v. a. 1. To look into the heart. 2. To study one's temper; ascertain one's inclinations; to sound one. Jigar kar chāk, qātil dekhtā thā, Jo pūchhā to kahā, 'dil dekhtā thā .' Nazīr. She pierced my heart and into it the murderess looked and peered, I said 'what's this?' she said 'I wished to see which way it veered. dil denā yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To give one's mind to; apply one's self in earnest. 2. ( dil hāth se jānā ). To give or lose one's heart to. 1. Maiṅ ne jo tujh se dil lagāyā, Kambakht! woh kaunsī ghaṛī thī? Wom. Rangīn. When I did fix my heart on you, you wretch! What luckless hour was it that did me fetch? 2. Dil jis se lagāyā woh huā dushman-i-jānī, Kuchh dil kā lagānā hī hameṅ rās na āyā . Wom. Jānī. On whomsoe'er my heart I fixed my foe became, The moment sure was unpropitious for my flame. dil-rubā ; H. man-lubhāū , adj. Heart-ravishing; bewitching. dil-rubā , n. m. A charmer; sweetheart. dil-rubāī , n. f. Allurement; ravishment; fascination. dil rakhnā , v. a. 1. To possess one's heart or affections. 2. To please; gratify; oblige. 3. To comfort; console. dil sā , better pān sā , ( Bot .) Cordate. dil-sard , adj. Cold-hearted; cold; indifferent. dil saṅbhālnā , v. n. To take courage; nerve oneself to an effort. dil-so/?/khtah , Poet. adj. Afflicted; grieved. dil-soz , adj. Affecting; touching; moving; distressing. dil-shikasta , Poet. adj. Broken-hearted; sorely afflicted. dil-fareb ; H. man-mohan , Poet. adj. Heart-alluring; fascinating; bewitching. dil kā bādshāh , Monarch of one's own heart. Ādmī apne dil kā bādshāh hai . Every one is king over his own heart. dil kā lahrī yā maujī , A merry jovial fellow. dil kaṛā yā kaṛvā k. , v. n. To summon courage or resolution; to bear up. Dil kaṛvā kar-ke davā pī jāo! dil-kash ; H. man-levā , adj. Attractive; winning; inviting; engaging; taking. dil-kushā , adj. Heart-expanding; exhilarating; cheering. dil ko lagnā , v. n. 1. To affect or touch one. 2. To approve itself to one; to seem right, reasonable. dil khaṭṭā k. , v. a. lit . to sour one's temper. To displease or offend one. dil ke phapole phoṛnā , v. a. To pay off old scores; take revenge ( jale phaphole phoṛnā ). Maë-khāne bīch jā ke shīshe tamām toṛe, Zāhid ne āj apne dil ke phapole phoṛe . Khān ābrū. The ale-house ent'ring he straightway each glass and bottle smashed, To day the man of God the gangrene of his soul hath squashed. sādā-dil , A simple or simple-hearted person. 1. Dilkī maiṅ sādi thī kambakht ki us se, annā, Na kīya maiṅ ne to māl o dil o īmān dareg . Wom. Rangīn. 2. Dil ki thī maiṅ sādī, jiskā pātī uskā khātī . Prov. So simple was I that I ate up all I could get. dil-gurdā , n. m. Courage ( bahādurī ). dil-gīr ; Rus. dal-gīr , adj. Melancholy. See اُداس 1. dil-gīrī , Illit. n. f. Melancholy; sorrow (اُداسي). dil lagnā , v. n. 1. To be attached to, fond of. 2. To be in love with or enamoured of. Dil lage kī yārī, Darogā! dil lage kī yārī! 3. To be entertained, amused, or pleased. dil-lagī , n. f. 1. Attachment; friendship. 2. Jest; joke; pleasantry. Khānā be-dil-lagī na pachtā thā, Melā ṭhelā koī na bachtā thā . Wom. Shauq. dil lenā , v. a. 1. To take one's heart away. Dil le līyā to ain ināyat hai yār kī, Achchhā hai! ranjisheṅ to gaiṅ bār bār kī . A favor great she hath shewn to me that she my heart hath took, For with it all my oft-felt heart griefs me have clean forsook. 2. To sound or get at one's heart. dil masos-kar rahnā, yā rah jānā , v. n. To wring one's heart and bear in silence. dil mailā k. , v. n. Wom. To take to heart; take on ( udās h .). dil meṅ pāp lānā , v. n. To cherish an improper sexual desire, or lust after one. Suno hamārī bāt, pāp na zarā dil meṅ lāo! Maiṅ to terā dharam-kā putr, tum mātā kahlāo . Rūp Basant. Now hear me, pray! let not this sin within thy heart, Thy step-son now am I, my mother called thou art! dil meṅ dil ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To put one's heart or mind in another. 2. To possess one's heart; to have influence over one. Ḍāl-kar dushman ke dil meṅ dil banā leṅ apnā dost, Par sitam yeh hai ki, zālim, tū hamāre dil meṅ hai . Arshad. A friend to make I fain within my foe's would put my heart, But then alas! thou, tyrant, in my heart hast lot and part! dil meṅ ḍālnā , v. a. To put into one's mind; to inspire. dil meṅ rakhnā , v. n. 1. To keep to oneself; to keep secret. 2. To bear in mind, to recollect. dil meṅ farq h. , v. n. To distrust; suspect. dil meṅ ghar k. , v. a. lit . to abide in one's heart. To take one's heart; win one's affections. 1. Dushman ke dil meṅ ghar karne ko hunar chāhīye . Prov. It needs skill to make a home in the heart of your foe. 2. Kīṛā zarā sā aur woh patthar meṅ ghar kare! Insān woh kyā na jo dil-i-dil-bar meṅ ghar kare . A worm can make its home within a stone, A man then in his mistress' heart a home might own. dil nashīṅ k. , v. a. To fix in the mind; impress upon. dil-vālā , 1. Bold; courageous. 2. Generous; liberal. dil o jāṅ , Heart and soul. dil o jān se , adv. With all one's heart and soul; heartily. dil o dimāg , Mind and heart; loftiness of soul; ambition. dil hī dil meṅ , adv. In one's inmost heart; to one's self; silently. be-dil , adj. 1. Unwilling; averse; dispirited. Be-dil naukar dushman barābar . Prov. An unwilling servant is a foe. 2. Adventurous; reckless ( bahādur ). be-dil , Poet. n. m. A lover; suitor. be-dilī , n. f. 1. Reluctance; unwillingness. 2. Discouragement; dejection. zindah-dil , adj. Sprightly; lively; merry; buxom; gay. murdah-dil , adj. Dull; cold; dead.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دِلْچَسْپ (dilčasp). By surface analysis, دِل (dil, “heart”) + چَسْپ (casp, “enjoined”).

def: s.m. The brain; head, mind, intellect; spirit; fancy, desire; airs, conceit; pride, haughtiness, arrogance; intoxication; high spirits (produced by stimulants, esp. by drinking bhang, &c.); — the organ of smell
more: n. m. 1. Brain; head; mind; intellect; genius. 2. Pride; haughtiness; arrogance. Karūṅ bistār kyā apnī dugānā kī rukhāī kā, Dimāg ā-kar unhīṅ meṅ ṭhus rahā sārī Khudāī kā . Inshā. My bed fellow's high dudgeon how describe? such pride! You'd think the world's pride all was stuffed in her inside. 3. The organ of smell. Zukām se dimāg band hai. dimāg pareshāṅ k. , v. a. To muddle one's brains; to produce a confused state of mind. dimāg jhaṛnā , v. n. 1. To run (the nose). 2. To have one's pride or nonsense taken out of him. dimāg chalnā, yā āsmān par h. , v. n. To be turned (one's head); to be exceedingly proud or haughty. Āj kal unkā dimāg chalā huā hai. dimāg khālī k. , v. a. To beat one's brains to no purpose. Chār ghanṭe dimāg khālī kīyā, magar khāk na samjhā. dimāg khālī h. , v. n. To feel as though the brains were out; to be addled; bored. dimāg-dār ; Wom. dimāg-pīṭī , adj. 1. Proud; …

(in Persian, also, damāg̠ẖ ) Pride, haughtiness, conceit, spirit, fancy. دماغ چڙهنا damāg̠ẖ chaṛhnā, To be proud with excess of wealth. دماغ کرنا damāg̠ẖ karnā, To be vain, proud, haughty; or rather, to act proudly, &c. دماغ هونا damāg̠ẖ honā, To be vain, haughty, proud.

def: s.m. Day (of twenty-four hours); a day (of twelve hours); daytime; time; circumstances; lot, fate; — (pl.) days, period of life, age
more: n. m. S. दिन, Pālī, dinam . 1. Day. 1. Ek din ke tīn sau sāṭh din haiṅ . Prov. One day my turn will come in days three hundred and sixty. 2. Din dūnī, rāt chaugnī! Prov. Saying. May your days be twice as long and your nights four times as long! (A blessing or a curse). 3. Abhī kaë din, kaë rāt? Prov. How many days and nights have yet passed over you? (It was but the other day). 4. Din īd, rāt shab-barāt . Prov. Each day was festive Id , and every night was Shab-barat (festive night). 5. Kabhī ke din baṛe, kabhī kī rāt . Prov. Now lengthens out the day, and now the night (The inconstancy of time.) 6. Woh din gaë, jo Khalīl Khāṅ fākhtah mārte the . Prov. The days are gone when Khalīl Khān shot doves. (Said to a do-nothing who plumes himself on the deeds of his ancestors). 7. Aiyām musībat ke to kāṭe nahīṅ kaṭte, Din aish kī ghaṛyoṅ ke guzar jāte haiṅ kaise? Shahīdī. The day of sorrow will …

wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi دن (dn /⁠din⁠/) (c. 1503), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀺𑀡 (diṇa), from Sanskrit दिन (diná), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *dyew-. Cognates Cognates include Punjabi دِݨ (diṇ) / دِن (din), Saraiki دِݨ (diṇ) and Sindhi ڏِنُ (ḍ̠iṇu).

s. See the value under درهم dirham; but, in the Qanoon-e-islam, it is described as equal to two and a half rupees only.

adj. Religious; pious. dīn-dārī n. f. Religiousness; piety. dīn dunyā , n. f. This world and the next; both worlds. dīn dunyā se jānā , v. n. To be deprived of happiness in both worlds. dīn dīn pukārnā , Mah. v. n. To raise the standard of a religious war. dīn kājhanḍā , n. m. Areligious flag or standard. dīn kā sāth denā , v. a. To fight for one's religion. dīn kī laṛāī n. f. A religious war; a crusade. dīn meṅ milānā , v. a. To convert to one's faith.
more: n. m. A debtor. dain wājib , n. m. A just debt.

def: n. m. An earthen vessel for drawing water from a well, usually attached to a Persian wheel.

n. f. Boasting; brag. ḍīṅg mārnā, yā hāṅknā, ḍīṅg kī lenā , v. a. To boast; brag. Khālī thā jo ḍhol ḍīṅg mārī, Par sonā bharā thā aur bhārī . Sohan Lāl. ḍīṅg'iyā, baṛaṅgyā , n. m. A braggard; boaster.

subs: ḍīng hāṅknā, v.n. To boast, brag.

wety: From Arabic دِينِيّ (dīniyy) (through Classical Persian دینی (dīnī)). By surface analysis, دین (dīn) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. Aged, old (generally applied to animals).
s.f. A day's wages, hire for the day, or by the day.
more: n. m. S. दण्ड stick. 1. A drum-stick. 2. A bass kettledrum; the bass end of the small kettle-drum. ḍaṅkā bajānā , v. a. 1. To beat a drum. 2. To reign; rule; command. 3. To exercise influence or authority. 4. To rejoice; make merry; revel in. 5. To make oneself a name. Pāpī bakhīl mat ban, dātā sakhī kahā jā, Ek dam tū apnā, ḍaṅkā man-māntā bajā jā! Nazīr. Be not a miser, sinner, leave a generous name behind! Strike strike some sympathetic chord before you leave your kind! ḍaṅkā bajnā , v. n. To be famous. Āj kal to unkā ḍankā baj rahā hai. ḍanke kī choṭ kahnā , v. a. 1. To proclaim by beat of drum. 2. To speak out or in public.

def: adj. Aged, old (generally applied to animals).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُنْیَا (dunyā), borrowed from Arabic دُنْيَا (dunyā), from أَدْنَى (ʔadnā), from دَنِيّ (daniyy). First attested in c. 1435 as Middle Hindi دنیا (dnya).

more: n. m. S. द्वीप Pālī, dīpo, dīpam . An island ( jazīrah ); a continent; region; country. The Hindu philosophers say that the terrestrial globe contains seven dīps or islands, encompassed by seven seas, the whole land and water measuring 7,957,752 yojans . dīp antar k. , v. n. To transport beyond seas.
n. m. Pālī dipo . A light ( chirā/?/ ).
s. m. An island; a continent, a region or clime of the earth.
s. m. A lamp. See ديا diyā.

def: s.m. A light, lamp
subs: s.m. 'Lamp-flowerʼ, the plant Michelia champaka (syn. ćampā)

s.m. A ceremony observed for ten days after the decease of a relative (it consists in the suspending of a lamp on a pīpal or some other tree for the purpose of lighting the departed spirit on the road to Yam-puri); — the offering a lamp to an idol; — land assigned to Brāhmans on the banks of rivers to deprecate river-encroachment
s.f. The flame of a lamp or candle
s.m. Lamp-black, the condensed smoke or soot of a lamp; snuff of a candle
s.f. A row of lamps, an illumination (see diwālī)
s.m. 'Lamp-tree'; the stand or stem of a lamp, candlestick.
wety: From Sanskrit दीप (dīpa).

def: adj. & s.m. Kindling, inflaming, illuminating, making bright or luminous; exciting, stimulating; — a light, lamp, candle; a kind of fire-work; — a rāg or musical mode sung at noon or at the dusk of evening in the hot season (the superstition is that singing it causes fire to break out); — the aromatic seed or plant Ptychotis ajowan or Ligustum ajaen
more: n. m. 1. A lamp (چراغ). Kul ko dīpak putr hai, aur mukh ko dīpak pān, Ghar ko dīpak istrī, dhaṛ ko dīpak pirān . Dohā. The light of the race is a son, the light of the lips is pān sole, The light of the house is the wife, the light of the body's the soul. 2. A kind of fire work. 3. A rāg , or musical mode sung at evening. 3. An air sung at dusk; an evening song, or hymn. dīpak chhand , A verse of four lines, each of 10 instants.
s. m. 1. A light, a candle, a lamp. 2. A kind of firework. 3. Name of a rāg, or musical mode, sung at noon in the hot weather.
subs: s.m. A verse of four lines, each of ten instants.
wety: From Sanskrit दीपक (dīpaka).

def: s.f. = diwālī, q.v. s.f. (lit. 'A row of lamps'), A Hindū festival celebrated on the day of the new moon of Kārttik in honour of Kārttikeya, the god of war. (At this festival, the Hindūs, after bathing in some river, put on their best attire, and perform a śrāddh; and at night they worship Lakshmī; their houses and streets are illuminated, and the night is spent in gambling.)
adj. (f. -ī) Kindling, inflaming, setting on fire, burning, causing light or heat; exciting, stimulating, digestive, tonic.
adj. corr. of dīpta, q.v. adj. Blazing, flaming, in flames; flashing, radiant, luminous, bright, glowing
subs: adj. corr. of dīpti-mān, q.v.s.v. dīpti.

def: n. m. 1. The lesser ( chhoṭī ) divālī , observed one day previous. 2. A ceremony observed for ten days after the decease of a relative It consists in suspending a lamp from a pipal or some other tree to light the departed spirit on the dark road to Jampuri. 3. Land assigned to Brahmans on the banks of rivers to deprecate river encroachments.
s.f. A light, lamp.
wety: From Sanskrit दीपिका (dīpikā).

def: n. f. A row of lamps; an illumination ( divālī ).
